summary.intent: Implement GitHub issue #254 ('Implement Accordion agnostic core') in crates/ars-components — a framework-agnostic state machine with typed focus effects, full test coverage, spec sync, and mutation audit, following the repo's TDD + post-implementation-audit workflow.
summary.outcome: The Accordion core was fully implemented with 45 unit tests, snapshot tests, spec-conformance, and property tests. A shared `navigation/key_token.rs` helper was extracted to eliminate duplication with Tabs. `cargo xci --profile fast` passed, the commit was made, and a PR was opened targeting main with `Closes #254`.

repo: Focus effects use a typed `Effect::FocusFocusedItem` contract — adapters read `Context::focused_item` and focus native handles — NOT a DOM-id `RequestFocus { target_id }` event.
`cargo xci --profile fast` is the required pre-push gate, running fmt/check/clippy/unit/integration/adapter stages in sequence.
`cargo xtask spec validate` validates spec files and must pass before review.
Commits require `Co-authored-by: OpenAI Codex <codex@openai.com>` in the message.
The `post-implementation-audit` and `waiting-for-codex-review` skills in `.agents/skills/` are mandatory closeout steps.
`INSTA_UPDATE=always` is the flag to accept new insta snapshots; snapshot changes require the `snapshot-reviewed` PR label.
Ignored `#[ignore]` proptests are run with `cargo test ... -- --ignored`.
GitHub project item status is updated via `gh api graphql` mutation with `updateProjectV2ItemFieldValue`.
`Bindable<T>` handles controlled/uncontrolled value patterns; initial state is `Bindable::controlled` when `Props::value = Some(_)`, otherwise uncontrolled from `default_value`.
`CollapseAll` must preserve already-open disabled items — the proptest invariant was initially too strict and had to be narrowed to avoid false failures.
Both Leptos and Dioxus adapter specs described passing adapter-owned `content_id`/`trigger_id` into core attr helpers — this was stale drift since the core now derives IDs itself.
`dom_safe_key_token` was duplicated in both `navigation::tabs` and `navigation::accordion`; extracted to `crates/ars-components/src/navigation/key_token.rs` as a private crate helper.

code.finding: The `End` key branch had a surviving mutant because no test verified that non-`End` keys cannot accidentally dispatch `FocusLast`; required a dedicated negative-key test.

`step_focus` reverse-navigation arithmetic (previous-item index) had surviving mutants — decrement needed a test that distinguishes it from increment/division by using a multi-item list with wrap-around assertion.

New shared helper for DOM-safe key tokens (i-/u-/s-hex patterns). Belongs in `ars-components` navigation, not on `Key` in `ars-collections`, because id-fragment encoding is a rendering concern, not collection identity.
Had a private `dom_safe_key_token` function that was the canonical reference implementation; now deleted in favor of the shared helper.

workflow: cargo-mutants prints misses immediately as they occur — prolonged silence during a run means no survivors so far, not a hang.
cargo-mutants requires `pkill -9 -f 'cargo-mutants|cargo mutants|mutants'` to forcibly stop; graceful SIGTERM is insufficient.
`cargo test` only accepts a single test-name filter string; combining multiple test names with spaces or commas silently runs no tests or errors.
Iterative mutation testing (run → find miss → patch → rerun) is effective but expensive at ~35 min/pass for a ~190-mutant file; plan for 3-6 passes on a new state machine.
Surviving mutants in guard combinations (disabled root + disabled item + non-collapsible) require tests that isolate each guard independently, not just combined-path tests.
The `sync_props_plan` normalization path needs tests that prove behavior when individual props change, not just a combined `SyncProps` event.

Property test invariants must account for initial state from `default_value` — a disabled item can legitimately start open, so the invariant should only block *new mutation events* from opening disabled items.

friction: cargo-mutants ran for ~35 minutes per pass and required 6+ iterations across the session to converge to zero survivors.
Mutation runs could not be interrupted cleanly — required `pkill -9` each time, losing partial progress artifacts.
Each mutation rerun discarded the `mutants.out/` directory, so intermediate miss diffs had to be reconstructed from streamed output before killing the process.
The `cargo llvm-cov` coverage run was slow enough to time out or stall the session while waiting for results.
`cargo test` filter syntax does not support multiple pattern arguments; the invalid multi-filter attempts silently failed or errored, requiring re-runs.
The GitHub GraphQL mutation for moving project items required multiple exploratory queries to find the correct project/field/option IDs before the status update could be issued.
open_items: Leptos and Dioxus adapter components for Accordion are explicitly deferred — no `ars-leptos` or `ars-dioxus` implementation exists yet.
Widget/example usage of Accordion is out of scope for this task.
PR CI must pass and Codex must leave 👍 before issue #254 can be closed — the task is not complete at commit time.
The `waiting-for-codex-review` skill must be invoked after each push to the PR branch per repo workflow.
The `snapshot-reviewed` label needs to be confirmed applied to the PR since new `.snap` files were created.
Other navigation components (Steps, Pagination, Link) may also benefit from the new `key_token` helper if they ever generate DOM-safe id fragments — not investigated in this task.

The accordion proptest coverage was narrowed to avoid false failures around disabled-item initial state; a more precise invariant distinguishing initial vs. mutation-driven open sets could be written as a follow-up.
